Okay, let me think carefully. The task requires me to cast a palm-sized object on a table. As I am right-handed, I should consider the most natural motion for casting with my dominant hand. Generally, when casting an object, the hand typically starts at a higher position (above the head) to gain momentum. The arm should be extended forward to provide the necessary propulsion. The force applied depends on the size of the object, but since it's palm-sized, moderate force might suffice. The primary vertical direction will be downward as the object is cast towards the table surface. The horizontal direction is mainly forward as I aim to direct the object accurately onto the table. Combining these observations, the action can be described as follows: "With my right hand, I start with my arm extended and positioned above my head, applying a moderate force to cast the palm-sized object downwards and forward onto the table."
/answer
